operating_state: Total revenue reached $8.1 billion in Q2 2023, up 20% year-over-year, driven by broad-based international growth and North America expansion. International markets generated nearly 80% of global revenue with strong sequential growth across Middle East and Asia. Well Construction led segment performance with significant year-over-year increases, followed by Production Systems growth, while Digital & Integration remained flat due to lower APS project revenue and reduced exploration data licensing.
cost_structure: Pretax segment operating margin expanded 264 basis points year-over-year to 19% driven by higher activity levels, improved pricing, and favorable activity mix toward international markets. Cost of services increased proportionally with revenue while maintaining margin expansion through operational leverage. Research & engineering expenses remained stable at 2.1% of revenue, while general & administrative expenses improved to 1.2% of revenue.
material_events: SLB completed the sale of its remaining Liberty Energy shares for $137 million net proceeds, recognizing a $36 million gain. The company issued $1 billion in new senior notes and repurchased $443 million of common stock under its $10 billion share repurchase program. SLB appointed James T. Hackett as independent Chair-elect effective July 2023.
macro_exposures: SLB halted all product and technology shipments to Russia from worldwide facilities in July 2023, expanding beyond previous geographic restrictions. Russia represented approximately 5% of worldwide revenue with net assets including receivables and fixed assets. The company maintains exposure to foreign currency fluctuations and uses cross-currency interest rate swaps to hedge Euro-denominated debt.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital investments of $2.5 to $2.6 billion for full year 2023 compared to $2.3 billion in 2022. The company anticipates continued international and offshore-led growth momentum in the second half while North America moderates as expected. SLB maintains $3.2 billion in cash and short-term investments plus $6.6 billion in unused committed credit facilities.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces ongoing risks from the Ukraine conflict's duration and escalation, which could affect regional economic conditions and trigger additional sanctions impacting global supply chains. SLB continues monitoring dynamic sanctions and trade control restrictions while assessing potential cybersecurity, regulatory, and reputational risks.
